title: sambucus cerulea f. glauca
intro: Sambucus cerulea f. glauca, also known as Blue Elderberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Blue Elderberry include its potential to reduce symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as its ability to support immune function and alleviate sinus pressure. Blue Elderberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural defense against illness.
